ragas.name: Nattai
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; associated with grandeur and heroism; 'Nata' and 'Nattai' are used interchangeably in the tradition

ragas.name: Gowla
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; evokes pathos and devotion

ragas.name: Arabhi
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; majestic and expansive character

ragas.name: Varali
ragas.notes: Janya of Tanarupi (6th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; considered auspicious yet difficult; associated with Ganesha

ragas.name: Sri
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; associated with grandeur and devotion; considered the crown raga of the Pancharatna set

ragas.name: Todi
ragas.notes: 8th melakarta; one of the most expansive ragas in the Carnatic canon; evokes pathos, longing, and deep introspection; a favourite for extended alapana

ragas.name: Bhairavi
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); distinct from Sindhu Bhairavi; evokes pathos and devotion; traditionally used to close concerts

ragas.name: Sindhu Bhairavi
ragas.notes: Distinct from Bhairavi; janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); popularised in the 20th century; lighter emotional register than Bhairavi; widely used in film music

ragas.name: Kalyani
ragas.notes: 65th melakarta (Mechakalyani); all sharp notes except natural Sa and Pa; associated with brightness, auspiciousness, and devotion

ragas.name: Shankarabharanam
ragas.notes: 29th melakarta; equivalent to the Western major scale; considered the most complete and balanced raga; parent of Nata and Arabhi

ragas.name: Kambhoji
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); one of the most popular ragas; associated with bhakti and grandeur; a staple of the concert repertoire

ragas.name: Begada
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); associated with devotion and serenity; a favourite of the Tyagaraja school

ragas.name: Saveri
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); evokes pathos and longing; associated with morning hours

ragas.name: Kedaram
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); associated with devotion and grandeur; a favourite for kritis in the Tyagaraja corpus

ragas.name: Natabhairavi
ragas.notes: 20th melakarta; parent of Bhairavi and Sindhu Bhairavi; evokes pathos and devotion; associated with evening hours

ragas.name: Ravichandrika

sources.label: Suja Music — Ravichandrika scale and kritis

ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); vakra raga — ārohana S R2 G3 M1 D2 N2 D2 S', avarohana S' N2 D2 M1 G3 R2 S; rare raga, sung mostly in early concert segments; best known for two Tyagaraja kritis: Makelara and Nirvadhi Sukhada

ragas.name: Surutti
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); bhāshāṅga vakra raga (Subbarama Dikshitar classification); ārohana S R₂ M₁ P N₂ D₂ N₂ Ṡ, avarohana Ṡ N₂ D₂ P M₁ G₃ P M₁ R₂ S; equivalent to Mixolydian mode; evokes devotion and pathos; a favourite raga of the Tyagaraja school

ragas.name: Reetigowla
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); vakra shadava-sampoorna raga (6 notes ascending, 7 descending); evokes bhakti mixed with joy; a favourite of the Tyagaraja school

ragas.name: Poorna Shadjam

sources.label: Dr. M. Radhakrishnan — Rare Raga Series: Poornashadjam

ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); oudava-shadava raga (5 notes ascending, 6 descending); ārohana S R2 G2 M1 N2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 P M1 G2 R2 S; rare raga popularised by the Tyagaraja school; principal compositions are Tyagaraja's 'Śrī māninī manōhara' and 'Lavanya Rāma'

ragas.name: Devamritavarshini
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); ārohana S R2 G2 M1 N2 D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G2 R2 S; distinct from Amritavarshini (which is a Dikshitar creation, janya of Chitrambari); associated with the Tyagaraja school

ragas.name: Sriraga
ragas.notes: Asampurna melakarta equivalent of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); audava-vakra-sampurna raga (5 notes ascending, zig-zag descending); no gandharam or dhaivatam in ascending scale; ancient raga; not related to the Hindustani raga Shree

ragas.name: Ahiri
ragas.notes: Janya of Vakulabharanam (14th melakarta); also associated with Hanumatodi (8th melakarta); sampurna raga with vakra swaras in ascending scale; evokes karuna rasa (compassion); considered an early-morning raga; ancient raga mentioned in Sangita Makarandha

ragas.name: Nadanamakriya

sources.label: Suja Music — Nadanamakriya scale and notes

ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); nishandantya raga (highest note is Nishada); ārohana S R1 G3 M1 P D1 N3, avarohana N3 D1 P M1 G3 R1 S N3; evokes a questioning, yearning, contemplative mood; associated with slow-tempo padams and meditative compositions

ragas.name: Gowlipantu
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); audava-vakra raga (5 notes ascending, vakra descending); ārohana S R₁ M₁ P N₃ S, avarohana S N₃ D₁ P M₁ D₁ M₁ G₃ R₁ S; associated with padams and javalis of the Kshetrayya tradition

ragas.name: Manirangu
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); audava-sampoorna raga (5 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 P M1 G2 R2 S; associated with Muttusvāmi Dīkṣita compositions

ragas.name: Devagandhari
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); evokes devotion and grandeur; associated with compositions of Tyagaraja

ragas.name: Khamas
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); bhashanga raga; evokes romance and longing; widely used in javalis and padams

ragas.name: Mayamalavagowla
ragas.notes: 15th melakarta; one of the first ragas taught to beginners; evokes pathos and devotion; parent of Nadanamakriya and Gowlipantu among others

ragas.name: Kharaharapriya
ragas.notes: 22nd melakarta; equivalent to the Dorian mode; parent of many important janya ragas including Reetigowla, Sriraga, Devamritavarshini, Manirangu, and Devagandhari; associated with devotion and grandeur

ragas.name: Kapi
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); bhashanga raga admitting both G2 and G3; evokes srngara and karuna; a favourite raga for padams and javalis; associated with the devadasi repertoire

ragas.name: Sahana
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); vakra raga; evokes pathos and longing; associated with padams and javalis of the Kshetrayya tradition; characteristic gamaka-laden phrases

ragas.name: Punnaga Varali
ragas.notes: Janya of Tanarupi (6th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending); ārohana S R1 M1 P D1 S, avarohana S D1 P M1 R1 S; evokes deep pathos and longing; a favourite for padams and javalis; associated with the Kshetrayya tradition

ragas.name: Lalitha
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); audava-shadava raga (5 notes ascending, 6 descending); ārohana S R1 G3 M1 P S, avarohana S N3 P M1 G3 R1 S; evokes tenderness and devotion; associated with Shyama Shastri's compositions, particularly 'Nannu Brovu'

ragas.name: Malavi
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending); ārohana S R1 M1 P N3 S, avarohana S N3 P M1 R1 S; evokes pathos and longing; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Nenarunchinanu'

ragas.name: Umaabharanam
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); shadava-audava raga (6 notes ascending, 5 descending); ārohana S R1 G3 M1 P D1 S, avarohana S N3 P M1 G3 R1 S; evokes devotion and grandeur; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Nijamarmamulanu'

ragas.name: Bahudari
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); audava-shadava raga (5 notes ascending, 6 descending); ārohana S R2 M1 P D2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 R2 S; evokes devotion and serenity; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Brova Bharama'

ragas.name: Kaanada
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); vakra raga with characteristic zigzag phrases; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 N2 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes grandeur and devotion; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Sri Narada'

ragas.name: Ananda Bhairavi
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); bhashanga vakra raga; ārohana S G2 R2 G2 M1 P D2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes srngara and karuna; a favourite for padams and javalis; associated with Sarangapani's compositions

ragas.name: Mohanam
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending, no M or N); ārohana S R2 G3 P D2 S, avarohana S D2 P G3 R2 S; evokes joy, beauty, and devotion; one of the most popular pentatonic ragas in the Carnatic canon

ragas.name: Harikambhoji
ragas.notes: 28th melakarta; equivalent to the Western Mixolydian mode; ārohana S R2 G3 M1 P D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S; parent of Kambhoji, Khamas, Surutti, Sahana, Mohanam, Bahudari, Kedaram, Begada, Ravichandrika, and Kapi among others; associated with bhakti and grandeur

ragas.name: Jayamanohari
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); shadava-sampoorna raga (6 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R2 G3 M1 D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S; evokes joy and devotion; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Yagnadulu Sukhamanu'

ragas.name: Hamsanadam
ragas.notes: Janya of Mechakalyani (65th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending); ārohana S G3 M2 P N3 S, avarohana S N3 P M2 G3 S; evokes grandeur and devotion; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Bantureeti Kolu'

ragas.name: Keeravani
ragas.notes: 21st melakarta; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P D1 N3 S, avarohana S N3 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S; equivalent to the Western harmonic minor scale; evokes pathos and longing; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Kaligiyunte'

ragas.name: Behag
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); bhashanga vakra raga admitting both M1 and M2; ārohana S G3 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 M1 R2 S; evokes romance and longing; a favourite raga for javalis and padams; associated with Patnam Subramanya Iyer's 'Samayamide Rara'

ragas.name: Sowrashtram
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); shadava-sampoorna raga (6 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R1 G3 M1 P N3 S, avarohana S N3 D1 P M1 G3 R1 S; evokes devotion and auspiciousness; traditionally used for mangalam compositions; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Ni Nama Roopamulaku'

ragas.name: Jayantasena

sources.label: Shivkumar.org — Vinata Suta Vahana notation (scale explicitly stated)

ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); shadava raga (6 notes ascending and descending, no Rishabha); ārohana S G2 M1 P D2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G2 S; a rare raga; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Vinatā Suta Vāhana'

ragas.name: Hamsadhwani
ragas.notes: Janya of Mechakalyani (65th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes, no Madhyama or Dhaivata); ārohana S R2 G3 P N3 S, avarohana S N3 P G3 R2 S; evokes brightness, auspiciousness, and joy; a favourite opening raga for concerts; best known for Dikshitar's 'Vatapi Ganapatim' and the jalajAksha varnam of Maanambuchaavadi Venkatasubbaiyer

ragas.name: Gundakriya
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending); ārohana S R1 G3 M1 N3 S, avarohana S N3 M1 G3 R1 S; evokes pathos and devotion; a rare raga used primarily in the early segments of a concert; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Intanuchu Varimpa'

ragas.name: Huseni
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); bhashanga vakra raga; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes pathos and longing; associated with padams and swarajathis; best known for Swati Tirunal's swarajathi 'Pahimam Brhannayike'

ragas.name: Simhendramadhyamam
ragas.notes: 57th melakarta; ārohana S R1 G1 M2 P D1 N3 S, avarohana S N3 D1 P M2 G1 R1 S; evokes pathos and devotion; a favourite raga of Papanasam Sivan; best known for his composition 'En Manam'

ragas.name: Suddha Saveri
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); audava raga (5 notes ascending and descending, no Gandhara or Nishada); ārohana S R2 M1 P D2 S, avarohana S D2 P M1 R2 S; evokes serenity and devotion; associated with compositions of Periyasami Tooran and the Tamil devotional repertoire

ragas.name: Manji
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); vakra raga; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 P D2 N2 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes pathos and longing; associated with Shyama Shastri's compositions, particularly 'Brovavamma Tamasame'

ragas.name: Bilahari
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); audava-sampoorna raga (5 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R2 G3 P D2 S, avarohana S N3 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S; evokes joy and auspiciousness; a favourite raga for thillanas; associated with Ariyakudi Ramanuja Iyengar's thillana

ragas.name: Pantuvarali
ragas.notes: 51st melakarta (Kamavardhini); ārohana S R1 G3 M2 P D1 N3 S, avarohana S N3 D1 P M2 G3 R1 S; evokes pathos and grandeur; associated with Tyagaraja's 'Aparama Bhakthi' and Dikshitar's 'Sri Satyanarayanam'; one of the most expansive melakartas for alapana

ragas.name: Ishamanohari
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); shadava-sampoorna raga (6 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R2 M1 P D1 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes devotion and serenity; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Manasa Sri Ramachandruni'

ragas.name: Dwijavanthi
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); bhashanga vakra raga; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G2 R2 S; evokes devotion and grandeur; best known for Muthuswami Dikshitar's 'Chetasri Balakrishnam'; a favourite raga of the Dikshitar school

ragas.name: Abheri
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); audava-sampoorna raga (5 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S G2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 D1 P M1 G2 S; evokes pathos and longing; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Nagumomu Ganaleni'; a favourite raga for the main piece in a concert

ragas.name: Shubapantuvarali
ragas.notes: Janya of Kamavardhini (51st melakarta); vakra raga; ārohana S R1 G3 M2 P M2 G3 N3 S, avarohana S N3 D1 P M2 G3 R1 S; evokes grandeur and devotion; best known for Muthuswami Dikshitar's 'Sri Satyanarayanam'; associated with the Dikshitar school

ragas.name: Sriranjini
ragas.notes: Janya of Mechakalyani (65th melakarta); audava-sampoorna raga (5 notes ascending, 7 descending); ārohana S R2 G3 M2 D2 S, avarohana S N3 D2 P M2 G3 R2 S; evokes romance and longing; a favourite raga for padams and javalis; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Brochevarevare'

ragas.name: Mukhari
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); sampurna raga with vakra phrases; ārohana S R2 G2 M1 P D1 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 G2 S; evokes pathos and devotion; one of the most expansive ragas for alapana; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Sarasiruhanana'

ragas.name: Nattakurinji
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); vakra raga; ārohana S R2 G3 M1 N2 D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S; evokes romance and longing; a favourite raga for the main piece in a concert; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Manasu Vishaya'

ragas.name: Vakulabharanam
ragas.notes: 14th melakarta; ārohana S R1 G2 M1 P D1 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R1 S; evokes pathos and devotion; a rare melakarta raga; best known for the composition 'Konniyada Tharama' attributed to Garbhapurivasa

ragas.name: Balahamsa
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); rare raga; best known for Tyagaraja's 'Viraja Turaga'; no standalone Wikipedia article confirmed

ragas.name: Atana
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta). Bold heroic character. Frequently used for Tyagaraja kritis.

ragas.name: Dhenuka
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta). Pentatonic, gentle devotional character.

ragas.name: Gowrimanohari
ragas.notes: 23rd melakarta (Kanakangi group). Sampurna raga, serene majestic character.

ragas.name: Hamir Kalyani
ragas.notes: Janya of Kalyani (65th melakarta). Corresponds to Hindustani Hamir. Bright expansive character.

ragas.name: Jonepuri
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta). Corresponds to Hindustani Jaunpuri. Melancholic contemplative character.

ragas.name: Kannada
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta). Vakra raga, majestic dignified character. Used in Dikshitar kritis.

ragas.name: Kannada Gowla
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta). Rare raga. Tyagaraja composed Sogasu Chooda in this raga.

ragas.name: Karnataka Kapi
ragas.notes: Janya raga distinct from Kapi. Used in padams and varnams. Swati Tirunal's Sumasaayaka is a well-known composition.

ragas.name: Kurinji
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta). Pentatonic, sweet romantic character.

ragas.name: Maund
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta). Corresponds to Hindustani Mand. Gentle folk-like character, popular for bhajans.

ragas.name: Nalinakanthi
ragas.notes: Janya of Shankarabharanam (29th melakarta). Pentatonic, bright cheerful character.

ragas.name: Neelambari
ragas.notes: Janya of Shankarabharanam (29th melakarta). Associated with lullabies and the divine. Dikshitar composed several kritis here.

ragas.name: Padi
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta). Rare raga used by Dikshitar. Shri Guruna Palitosmi is a well-known kriti.

ragas.name: Purvi
ragas.notes: Corresponds to Hindustani Purvi. Serious evening raga, contemplative character. Used by Dikshitar.

ragas.name: Simhavahini
ragas.notes: Janya of Shankarabharanam (29th melakarta). Rare raga. Tyagaraja's Nenarunchara is a well-known kriti.

ragas.name: Thodi
ragas.notes: 8th melakarta (Todi group). One of the most important ragas in Carnatic music. Deep emotive character, parent of many janya ragas.

ragas.name: Yadukulakhamboji
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta). Pentatonic, bright devotional character. Tyagaraja's Hecharikaga is a well-known kriti.

ragas.name: Yaman Kalyan
ragas.notes: Corresponds to Hindustani Yaman Kalyan. Related to Carnatic Kalyani. Used for bhajans and devotional music.

ragas.name: Yamuna Kalyani
ragas.notes: Janya of Kalyani (65th melakarta). Pentatonic, bright devotional character. Dikshitar's Jambu Pathe Mam Pahi is in this raga.

ragas.name: Gambhira Nattai
ragas.notes: Janya of Mayamalavagowla (15th melakarta). Rare raga used in Mallaris and temple music. Majestic, ceremonial character.

ragas.name: Darbari Kanada
ragas.notes: Corresponds to Hindustani Darbari Kanada. Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta). Deep, serious, late-night raga. Used in Carnatic music for devotional and Shiva-related compositions.

ragas.name: Poorvikalyani
ragas.notes: Janya of Kalyani (65th melakarta); pentatonic (audava-audava) raga; arohana S R2 G3 P N3 S, avarohana S N3 P G3 R2 S; evokes devotion and serenity; frequently used in Tyagaraja kritis

ragas.name: Chakravakam
ragas.notes: 16th melakarta raga; arohana S R1 G3 M1 P D2 N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R1 S; evokes pathos and devotion; a favourite of Tyagaraja who composed several kritis in this raga

ragas.name: Narayanagowla
ragas.notes: Janya raga derived from Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); arohana S R2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 P M1 G2 R2 S; a serious, contemplative raga used by Muthuswami Dikshitar and Tyagaraja

ragas.name: Sama
ragas.notes: Janya raga of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); arohana S R2 M1 P N2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S (vakra); a gentle, devotional raga; Patnam Subramanya Iyer composed Maravakave O Manasa in this raga

ragas.name: Vasantha
ragas.notes: Janya raga of Mechakalyani (65th melakarta); arohana S G3 R2 G3 M2 D2 N3 S, avarohana S N3 D2 M2 G3 R2 S (vakra); evokes spring and devotion; Sri Kamakshi Katakshi attributed to Subbaraya Sastri is set in this raga

ragas.name: Devamanohari
ragas.notes: Janya raga of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); arohana S R2 G3 P D2 S, avarohana S N2 D2 P G3 R2 S; a gentle, lyrical raga; Tyagaraja's Kanna Tandri Napai is set in this raga

ragas.name: Tanarupi
ragas.notes: 6th melakarta raga, 6th raga of the 1st cakra (Indu). Parent mela of Punnaga Varali (parentage contested — some sources cite Natabhairavi as bhashanga parent).

compositions.title: Jagadananda Karaka
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; opens the Pancharatna set; a paean to Rama in the majestic Ghana raga Nata

compositions.title: Dudukugala
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; a self-critical composition in the pathos-laden Ghana raga Gowla

compositions.title: Sadhinchane
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; a devotional composition in the majestic Ghana raga Arabhi

compositions.title: Kana Kana Ruchira
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; set in the auspicious and technically demanding Ghana raga Varali

compositions.title: Entharo Mahanubhavulu
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; considered the crown jewel of the Tyagaraja corpus; a salutation to all great souls; traditionally sung last in the Pancharatna set

compositions.title: maakElaraa vicaaramu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in the rare raga Ravichandrika; one of the two principal compositions that define this raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Gitarthamu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Surutti; a meditation on the meaning of the Gita

compositions.title: Bālē Bālēndu Bhūṣaṇi
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Reetigowla; addressed to the Mother at Tiruvaiyaru; pallavi uses the epithet 'bālē' (O Mother)

compositions.title: Abhimānamenneḍu
compositions.notes: Kriti by Patnam Subramanya Iyer in Begada

compositions.title: Bhajana Parulakēla
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Surutti; set to Rupakam tala

compositions.title: Śrī Māninī Manōhara

sources.label: Thyagaraja Vaibhavam blog — notation and meaning

compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in the rare raga Poorna Shadjam; Tyagaraja pleads with the Lord to grant him service at His feet

compositions.title: Enduku Peddala
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Shankarabharanam

compositions.title: Alarśara Paritāpam
compositions.notes: Padam by Swati Tirunal in Surutti; misra capu tala

compositions.title: Karuṇā Samudrā
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Devagandhari

compositions.title: Marubārī Tāḷalēnurā
compositions.notes: Jāvaḷi by Tiruppaṇandāḷ Paṭṭābhirāmaiyyā in Khamas; one of his most popular compositions; mudra: Talavam

compositions.title: Evarani Nirṇayiñcirirā
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Devamritavarshini; Tyagaraja asks who can determine the nature of the Lord

compositions.title: Nāma Kusumamulacē Pūjiñcē
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Sriraga

compositions.title: Ēṭi Janmamidi
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Varali; misra capu tala

compositions.title: Cakkani Rājamārgamulu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kharaharapriya

compositions.title: Koluvamaregadā
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Todi; a devotional composition asking whether the Lord is pleased

compositions.title: Lalitē Kāñci Nagara Nivāsinī
compositions.notes: Kriti by Annasami Shastri in Bhairavi; addressed to the goddess of Kanchipuram

compositions.title: Callarē Rāmacandruni
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Ahiri; misra capu tala

compositions.title: Cittam Eppaḍiyō
compositions.notes: Tamil composition by Mayuram Vedanayagam Pillai (Samuel Vedanayagam Pillai) in Nadanamakriya; misra capu tala

compositions.title: Kṣīra Sāgara Śayana
compositions.notes: Composition by Maharaja Jayachamarajendra Wadiyar of Mysore in Mayamalavagowla; caturaśra dhruva tala

compositions.title: Kuvalayākṣirō
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Gowlipantu; misra capu tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition

compositions.title: Māmava Paṭṭābhirāma
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muttusvāmi Dīkṣita in Manirangu; misra capu tala; addressed to Lord Pattabhirama

compositions.title: Telisenura
compositions.notes: Padam by Chinnaiah Pillai of the Tanjore Quartet in Saveri; Rupakam tala; part of the devadasi padam repertoire

compositions.title: Adiyokka Yugamu
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Kambhoji; Tisra Triputa tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition addressed to Muvvagopala

compositions.title: Parulanna Matta
compositions.notes: Javali by Dharmapuri Subbaraya Iyer in Kapi; Rupakam tala; part of the javali repertoire of the devadasi tradition

compositions.title: Mora Topu
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Sahana; Tisra Triputa tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition

compositions.title: Pusadaramu
compositions.notes: Javali by Govindasvamy Ayya in Todi; Tisra Triputa tala

compositions.title: Narimani
compositions.notes: Javali by Dharmapuri Subbaraya Iyer in Khamas; Adi tala

compositions.title: Yala Padare
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Begada; Tisra Triputa tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition

compositions.title: Ninnu Juchi
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Punnaga Varali; Tisra Triputa tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition

compositions.title: Intha Chalamu
compositions.notes: Varnam by Veenai Kuppayyar in Begada; Adi tala; part of the standard veena varnam repertoire

compositions.title: Himadri Sute
compositions.notes: Kriti by Shyama Shastri in Kalyani; Rupakam tala; addressed to the goddess Kamakshi

compositions.title: Nannu Brovu
compositions.notes: Kriti by Shyama Shastri in Lalitha; Mishra Chapu tala; a supplication to the goddess Kamakshi; one of the principal compositions defining raga Lalitha

compositions.title: Nenarunchinanu
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Malavi; Deshadi tala; a meditation on the Lord's grace

compositions.title: Rama Nee Samanamevaru
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Kharaharapriya; Rupakam tala; a declaration that none equals Rama

compositions.title: Nijamarmamulanu
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Umaabharanam; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining raga Umaabharanam

compositions.title: Brova Bharama
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Bahudari; Deshadi tala; Tyagaraja asks whether it is a burden to protect him; one of the principal compositions defining raga Bahudari

compositions.title: Sri Narada
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Kaanada; Rupakam tala; a salutation to the sage Narada

compositions.title: Sri Raghuvara
compositions.notes: Kriti by Tyagaraja in Bhairavi; Adi tala; a devotional composition addressed to Rama

compositions.title: Pattakura
compositions.notes: Padam by Sarangapani in Ananda Bhairavi; Rupakam tala; part of the Sarangapani padam tradition

compositions.title: Vadiga Gopaluni
compositions.notes: Padam by Kshetrayya in Mohanam; Misra Chapu tala; part of the Kshetrayya padam tradition addressed to Muvvagopala

compositions.title: Yagnadulu Sukhamanu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Jayamanohari; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Enduku Nirdaya
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Harikambhoji; Adi tala

compositions.title: Bantureeti Kolu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Hamsanadam; Deshadi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga

compositions.title: Kaligiyunte
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Keeravani; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga

compositions.title: Samayamide Rara
compositions.notes: Javali by Patnam Subramanya Iyer in Behag; Rupakam tala; one of the most celebrated javalis in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Ni Nama Roopamulaku
compositions.notes: Mangalam by Tyagaraja in Sowrashtram; Adi tala; traditionally sung to close a concert

compositions.title: Vinatā Suta Vāhana
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Jayantasena; Adi tala; pallavi 'Vinatā Suta Vāhana Śrī Ramaṇā'; a meditation on the futility of life without the Lord's darshana and the virtue of sat-sangati (company of the pious); one of the principal compositions defining raga Jayantasena in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: jalajākṣi ninnēḍabāsi
compositions.notes: Ata-tala varnam by Maanambuchaavadi Venkatasubbaiyer in Hamsadhwani; Adi tala; pallavi 'jalajakshi ninnedabasi chala marulu konnadira'; mudra 'Venkatesa'; one of the most frequently performed varnams in the concert repertoire; the karnatik.com source notes the composer took varnamettu-s left by Tyagaraja and fitted his own words

compositions.title: Śaṅkarī Nīvē
compositions.notes: Kriti by Subbaraya Sastri in Begada; Rupakam tala; pallavi 'śaṅkarī nīvē (amba) śaṅkarī nīvani ninnu santatamu kōri vacciti'; addressed to the goddess Kamakshi of Kanchipuram; one of the principal compositions of the Shyama Shastri lineage in Begada

compositions.title: entō prēmatō
compositions.notes: Ata-tala varnam by Veenai Kuppayyar in Surutti; Adi tala; one of the most frequently performed varnams in the Carnatic concert repertoire; pallavi 'entō prēmatō'; a standard varnam taught across all schools

compositions.title: entō prēmatōnu
compositions.notes: Varnam by Lalgudi Jayaraman in Bahudari; Adi tala; distinct from the Veenai Kuppayyar varnam of the same name in Surutti; part of the Lalgudi varnam corpus

compositions.title: Viriboni
compositions.notes: Ata-tala varnam by Pacchimiriyam Adiapayya in Bhairavi; one of the most celebrated varnams in the Carnatic repertoire; traditionally placed at the opening of a concert; pallavi 'viriboni bhāvamulanu'

compositions.title: Intanuchu Varimpa
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Gundakriya; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this rare raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Pahimam Brhannayike
compositions.notes: Swarajathi by Swati Tirunal in Huseni; Rupakam tala; authorship disputed in some scholarly sources; one of the most celebrated swarajathis in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: En Manam
compositions.notes: Tamil kriti by Papanasam Sivan in Simhendramadhyamam; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga in the Tamil devotional repertoire

compositions.title: Thaye Tripura
compositions.notes: Tamil kriti by Periyasami Tooran in Suddha Saveri; Khanda Chapu tala; addressed to the goddess Tripurasundari

compositions.title: Akshaya Linga Vibho
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muthuswami Dikshitar in Shankarabharanam; Misra Chapu tala; addressed to Lord Shiva as Akshayalinga; one of the principal Dikshitar compositions in this raga

compositions.title: Brovavamma Tamasame
compositions.notes: Kriti by Shyama Shastri in Manji; Misra Chapu tala; a supplication to the goddess; one of the principal compositions defining raga Manji in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Kandu Dhanyanade
compositions.notes: Kannada devaranama by Kamalesha Vittala Dasa in Behag; Adi tala; part of the Haridasa devotional repertoire

compositions.title: Azhagu Vadivai Vandhu
compositions.notes: Tamil virutham (free-verse devotional poem) sung as a ragamalika; no single raga or tala; composer not definitively established in standard references; frequently sung in the Tamil devotional concert tradition

compositions.title: Jalaja Bandhu
compositions.notes: Padam by Swati Tirunal in Surutti; Misra Chapu tala; distinct from 'Alarśara Paritāpam' though both are Swati Tirunal padams in Surutti; frequently paired with 'Alasara Parithapam' in concert performance

compositions.title: Thillana in Bilahari
compositions.notes: Thillana by Ariyakudi Ramanuja Iyengar in Bilahari; Adi tala; one of the most celebrated thillanas in the concert repertoire; a signature composition of the Ariyakudi school

compositions.title: Ninnu Kori
compositions.notes: Varnam by Ramnad Sreenivasa Iyengar in Mohanam; Adi tala; pallavi 'ninnu kori yunna nura nikila loka nayaka'; mudra 'Sreenivasa'; one of the standard Mohanam varnams in the concert repertoire; note: the tracklist of the Jamshedpur 1961 concert attributes this to 'Poochi Srinivasa Iyengar' — the shivkumar.org notation source explicitly names Ramnad Sreenivasa Iyengar as composer

compositions.title: Aparama Bhakthi
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Pantuvarali (Kamavardhini); Rupakam tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Suryamurte
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muthuswami Dikshitar in Sowrashtram; Dhruva tala; addressed to the Sun deity; part of the Navagraha Kritis cycle

compositions.title: Manasa Sri Ramachandruni
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Ishamanohari; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Vasudevayani
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kalyani; Adi tala; a devotional composition addressed to Vasudeva (Krishna); one of the standard Kalyani kritis in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Chetasri Balakrishnam
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muthuswami Dikshitar in Dwijavanthi; Thisra Ekam tala; addressed to the child Krishna; one of the principal compositions defining raga Dwijavanthi in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Thaye Yashodha
compositions.notes: Tamil composition attributed to Oothukadu Venkata Kavi in Todi; Adi tala; a vision of the child Krishna in Yashoda's courtyard; authorship disputed in scholarly literature — some sources attribute it to anonymous folk tradition; the neraval on 'kalinil silambu' is one of the most celebrated moments in the Carnatic concert repertoire

compositions.title: Nagumomu Ganaleni
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Abheri; Adi tala; pallavi 'nagumomu ganaleni natajana manamu'; one of the most celebrated kritis in the Carnatic repertoire; a meditation on the beauty of Rama's face; the principal composition defining raga Abheri in the concert tradition

compositions.title: Sri Satyanarayanam
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muthuswami Dikshitar in Shubapantuvarali; Thisra Ekam tala; addressed to Lord Satyanarayana; one of the principal compositions defining raga Shubapantuvarali in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Sami Ninne Kori
compositions.notes: Varnam by Karur Devudu Iyer in Sri raga; Adi tala; one of the standard Sri raga varnams in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Sri Maha Ganapathim
compositions.notes: Kriti by Muthuswami Dikshitar in Gowla; Misra Chapu tala; addressed to Ganesha; one of the principal Dikshitar compositions in this raga

compositions.title: Brochevarevare
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Sriranjini; Adi tala; pallavi 'brochevarevare ra ninu vina'; a supplication asking who else will protect him; the principal composition defining raga Sriranjini in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Sarasiruhanana
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Mukhari; Adi tala; pallavi 'sarasiruhanana'; one of the principal compositions defining raga Mukhari in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Ide Bhagyamu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kaanada; Misra Chapu tala; pallavi 'ide bhagyamu'; a meditation on the fortune of devotion to Rama

compositions.title: Lavanya Rama
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Poorna Shadjam; Adi tala; one of the two principal compositions defining this rare raga in the concert repertoire (alongside 'Sri Manimi Manohara')

compositions.title: Manasu Vishaya
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Nattakurinji; Adi tala; the principal composition defining raga Nattakurinji in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Konniyada Tharama
compositions.notes: Kriti by Garbhapuri Srinivasa (ankita: Garbhapurivasa) in Vakulabharanam; Adi tala; one of the principal compositions defining this rare melakarta raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Viraja Turaga
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Balahamsa; Adi tala; the principal composition defining this rare raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Apadooruku
compositions.notes: Javali by Tiruppaṇandāḷ Paṭṭābhirāmaiyyā in Khamas; Adi tala; distinct from 'Marubari Talalenu' by the same composer

compositions.title: Sirsirakkumeni (Thirupugazh)
compositions.notes: Thirupugazh by Arunagirinathar; Khanda Triputa tala; performed as a ragamalika (raga_id null as no single raga applies); a devotional hymn to Murugan

compositions.title: Thillana in Behag
compositions.notes: Thillana by Lalgudi Jayaraman in Behag; Adi tala; part of the celebrated Lalgudi thillana corpus; performed at the Music Academy December Season 1965

compositions.title: Emaanadicchevo
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Sahana raga.

compositions.title: Manavinaalakincha Raadate
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Nalinakanthi raga.

compositions.title: Sogasu Chooda
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kannada Gowla raga.

compositions.title: Hecharikaga
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Yadukulakhamboji raga.

compositions.title: Nadopasana
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Begada raga. Devotional composition on the worship of music.

compositions.title: Jagadanandakaraka
compositions.notes: First of Tyagaraja's Pancharatna Kritis. Nattai raga, Adi tala.

compositions.title: Endaro Mahanubhavulu
compositions.notes: Fifth of Tyagaraja's Pancharatna Kritis. Sri raga, Adi tala. Tribute to great souls.

compositions.title: Chakkani Raja Margamu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kharaharapriya raga.

compositions.title: Mohana Rama
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Mohanam raga.

compositions.title: Meru Samaana
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Mayamalavagowla raga.

compositions.title: Yee Vasudha
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Kanada raga.

compositions.title: Muripemu Kalige
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Mukhari raga.

compositions.title: Nenarunchara
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Simhavahini raga. Rare raga composition.

compositions.title: Siva Siva Siva Ena Radha
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Pantuvarali raga. Devotional composition on Shiva.

compositions.title: Guruleka Etuvanti
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Gowrimanohari raga. On the importance of a guru.

compositions.title: Laavanya Rama Kannul Ara
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Poorna Shadjam raga.

compositions.title: Teliyaleru Rama
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Dhenuka raga.

compositions.title: Sri Venkata Girisa
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Suruti raga.

compositions.title: Shri Guruna Palitosmi
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Padi raga. Rare raga composition.

compositions.title: Sri Dakshinamurte
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Shankarabharanam raga.

compositions.title: Amba Nilayatakshi Karunakatakshi
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Neelambari raga. Part of the Kamalamba Navavarana Kritis.

compositions.title: Kshitija Ramanam
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Devagandhari raga.

compositions.title: Jambu Pathe Mam Pahi
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Yamuna Kalyani raga. Pancha Bhuta Stalas series (water element).

compositions.title: Bhajare Re Chitta Balambikam
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Kalyani raga.

compositions.title: Sri Matrubhutam
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Kannada raga.

compositions.title: O Jagadamba
compositions.notes: Shyama Shastri kriti in Ananda Bhairavi raga. One of the Trinity's most celebrated compositions.

compositions.title: Kamakshi
compositions.notes: Shyama Shastri kriti in Varali raga. A popular concert piece.

compositions.title: Kanaka Shaila Viharini
compositions.notes: Shyama Shastri kriti in Punnagavarali raga.

compositions.title: Sarojadala Netri
compositions.notes: Shyama Shastri kriti in Shankarabharanam raga.

compositions.title: Sumasaayaka
compositions.notes: Swati Tirunal varnam in Karnataka Kapi raga.

compositions.title: Saramaina Matalento
compositions.notes: Swati Tirunal kriti in Behag raga.

compositions.title: Jagadodharana
compositions.notes: Purandara Dasa composition in Kapi raga. Popular devotional song on Krishna.

compositions.title: Baro Krishnayya
compositions.notes: Kanaka Dasa composition in Maund raga. Popular devotional song on Krishna.

compositions.title: Krishna Nee Begane
compositions.notes: Vyasatirtha composition in Yamuna Kalyani raga. One of the most popular Carnatic devotional songs.

compositions.title: Bare Panduranga
compositions.notes: Tukaram abhang in Maund raga. Popular Varkari devotional composition.

compositions.title: Tiruvatriyur Thyagarajan
compositions.notes: Ghanam Krishna Iyer composition in Atana raga.

compositions.title: Brocevarevarura Ninnu Vina Raghuvara
compositions.notes: Mysore Vasudevacharya kriti in Khamas raga. A popular concert piece.

compositions.title: Eppo Varuvaaro
compositions.notes: Gopalakrishna Bharathi composition in Jonepuri raga. A popular Tamil devotional song.

compositions.title: Muddugare Yashoda
compositions.notes: Annamacharya composition in Kurinji raga. A popular devotional song on Krishna and Yashoda.

compositions.title: Gajavadhana
compositions.notes: Kumara Ettendra composition in Thodi raga. A devotional composition on Ganesha.

compositions.title: Maithrim Bhajata
compositions.notes: Sanskrit composition by the Kanchi Paramacharya. First performed by MS Subbulakshmi at the UN in 1966.

compositions.title: Sri Ramachandra Kripalu
compositions.notes: Tulsidas bhajan in Yamuna Kalyani raga. A popular devotional composition on Rama.

compositions.title: Aaguner Parashmoni Chhoao Praane
compositions.notes: Rabindranath Tagore composition. Performed by TM Krishna in Bhairavi raga.

compositions.title: Yarukkaghilum Bhayamaa
compositions.notes: Subbarama Iyer padam in Begada raga, Misra Chapu tala.

compositions.title: Vande Meenakshi Twam
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar nottuswara in Shankarabharanam raga. Part of the Nottuswara Sahitya series composed to Western tunes. Dedicated to Meenakshi of Madurai.

compositions.title: Dhana Dhanya Pushpa Bhara
compositions.notes: Bengali patriotic song by Dwijendralal Roy. Performed by TM Krishna in concert as a tribute to Bengal. Not assigned to a specific Carnatic raga.

compositions.title: Ee Vasudha
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Sahana raga. Distinct from 'Yee Vasudha' (id: yee_vasudha) which is set in Kanada. Both share the same opening words; the Sahana version is confirmed at karnatik.com c2369.

compositions.title: Paraloka Sadhaname
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Poorvikalyani raga, Deshadi tala.

compositions.title: Sugunamule Cheppukonti
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Chakravakam raga, Rupakam tala.

compositions.title: Emi Jesithe
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Thodi raga, Misra Chapu tala.

compositions.title: Paramukham Elara
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Surutti raga, Adi tala.

compositions.title: Deva Deva Kalayamide
compositions.notes: Swati Tirunal kriti in Mayamalavagowla raga, Rupakam tala; a popular concert opener

compositions.title: Dinamani Vamsa
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Harikambhoji raga, Adi tala

compositions.title: Sri Ramam
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Narayanagowla raga, Adi tala; part of the Rama Navami series

compositions.title: Tali Ninnunera
compositions.notes: Shyama Shastri kriti in Kalyani raga, Misra Chapu tala

compositions.title: Marubalka
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Sriranjini raga, Adi tala; one of the most celebrated kritis in this raga

compositions.title: Vadanyeshwaram Bhajeham
compositions.notes: Muthuswami Dikshitar kriti in Devagandhari raga, Adi tala; on Vadanyeshwara (Shiva at Vaitheeswaran Koil)

compositions.title: Maravakave O Manasa
compositions.notes: Patnam Subramanya Iyer kriti in Sama raga, Rupakam tala

compositions.title: Sri Kamakshi Katakshi
compositions.notes: Attributed to Subbaraya Sastri in Vasantha raga, Adi tala; authorship disputed — some sources attribute to Syama Shastri

compositions.title: Kanna Tandri Napai
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Devamanohari raga, Deshadi tala

compositions.title: Solla Vallayo
compositions.notes: Subramanya Bharathi Tamil song; performed as a ragamalika in concert; raga_id null as no single raga is assigned

compositions.title: Ennaga Manasuku Rani
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Neelambari raga, Adi tala; a lullaby-like kriti often sung as a concert closer
